mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and household credit balances indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of INDPRO and RSAFS shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.10), or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, an acceleration in real GDP growth signals resilient economic momentum that overrides discount rate headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 accelerates and FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS drops below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor demand expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05, or forward returns are not statistically higher in months with payroll growth exceeding 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.4 million units annually, it indicates a sharp deceleration in residential investment and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative following HOUST readings below 1.40 million, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in > 55% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in industrial production and inflation indicates supply-constrained demand that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-product term of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows no significant predictive power for 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.05), or forward returns are not systematically negative when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion where RSAFS exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 10%, equity markets price in higher future cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when RSAFS breaches the threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 3-year walk-forward test.
